article_title: Wind and Solar Generate More Electricity Than Gas Worldwide for the First Time, Ember Data Shows

content_preview.title: Wind and Solar Generate More Electricity Than Gas Worldwide for the First Time, Ember Data Shows
content_preview.summary: In April 2026, wind and solar produced 531 TWh globally versus gas's 477 TWh — the first month ever that renewables topped gas in global electricity generation.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

For the first time in recorded history, wind and solar power generated more electricity worldwide than natural gas in a single calendar month. According to analysis published by British energy think tank Ember, wind and solar produced a combined 531 terawatt-hours (TWh) of electricity globally in April 2026, compared with 477 TWh from gas-fired power plants — a margin of 54 TWh — as reported by [Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/05/20/in-a-first-wind-solar-generated-more-power-than...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with a clear milestone lead, quantitative depth in the 'What We Know' section, appropriate epistemic humility in 'What We Don't Know', and a grounded analytical conclusion. The 755-word count sits well within the News category range. Internal cross-link to the prior US-only milestone is editorially useful and clearly marked.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four snapshots successfully fetched (source-0 through source-3, all HTTP 200). Source-0 (Electrek, source-0.html.gz): confirmed 531 TWh wind+solar vs 477 TWh gas in April 2026, 22% vs 20% share, 13% YoY growth, all country breakdowns (China +14%, EU +13%, UK +35%, US +8%, Australia +17%, Chile +24%, Brazil +4%), Kostantsa Rangelova quote verbatim, April seasonality caveat, and no coal-switching finding. Source-1 (Carbon Brief 'clean energy pushes fossil fuel power into reverse', source-1.html.gz): confirmed fossil fuel generation fell 0.2% in 2025, coal dropped 63 TWh to 10,476 TWh, solar grew 636 TWh (33% above prior record), the 1919 coal/renewables parity reference, and wind+solar meeting 99% of demand growth. Source-2 (Carbon Brief 'wind and solar saved UK from gas imports worth £1.7bn', source-2.html.gz): confirmed UK avoided £1.7bn in gas imports, 34 LNG tankers, 41 TWh avoided gas consumption, and gas generation in UK fell to record lows in March and April 2026. Source-3 (CleanTechnica/IEA 'age of electricity', source-3.html.gz): confirmed battery storage as fastest-growing power sector technology, approximately 110 GW new capacity, and costs dropping 45% in 2025. All claims in the article map to their cited sources without misattribution.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every quantitative claim traces directly to a cited source snapshot. No hallucinated quotes or fabricated specifics detected. The Kostantsa Rangelova quote is reproduced verbatim from the Electrek snapshot. The distinction between the April 2026 monthly milestone and the as-yet-unreached annual crossover is correctly stated and sourced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately qualifies the seasonal and crisis-driven factors that may have contributed to the April figure. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Not a duplicate. The April 16, 2026 article covered the US-only milestone in March 2026; this article covers the global milestone in April 2026. Different scope, different month, and explicitly cross-links to the earlier piece. The May 2026 archive contains no prior coverage of the global Ember data point.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean submission. All automated checks passed, all four source snapshots verified, no fabrications or misattributions found. The article responsibly contextualizes the April seasonality and the LNG supply disruption as contributing factors. Ready to publish as-is.
